mnemonics.title: E-R Model Key Components
mnemonics.content:
E.R.M. = Entities + Relationships + Attributes
Remember: 'Everything Requires Meaningful Associations' - Entities need Attributes to describe them and Relationships to connect them.

mnemonics.title: Attribute Types Memory Trick
mnemonics.content:
Simple/Single - One value, one oval
Composite - Split oval, multiple parts inside
Multivalued - Double oval, multiple values
Derived - Dashed oval, calculated
Key - Underlined oval, unique ID

Think: 'SCoMP DKi' = Simple, Composite, multivalued, Primary/derived, Key, underlined

acronyms.usage: Used in database design to represent the conceptual schema of a system
The graphical representation of entities, attributes, and relationships
A key attribute that uniquely identifies each entity in an entity set
An attribute that creates a relationship between tables by referencing a primary key

visualTips.visualization: Think of entities as NOUNS - people, places, things, or concepts. Rectangle = the 'box' that holds all information about one type of object.
Attributes are the ADJECTIVES that describe entities - they tell us 'what kind' or 'which one'. Oval shapes 'grow' out of the rectangle like characteristics.
Relationships are VERBS - actions between entities. Diamond shape shows the 'action' connecting two rectangles.
Use crow's foot notation: |< = one, >|< = many. Think of '|<' as one (single line) and '>{' as many (foot like a bird's foot with 3 toes).

analogies.analogy: Entity is like one specific student (John), while Entity Set is like the collection of all students in a college - the class or category.
Think of a weak entity like a dependent on an insurance policy - they can't exist independently without the primary policy holder (strong entity).
Total participation is like membership required to enter a club (you MUST have membership). Partial is like having a loyalty card (optional).
1:1 is like one person has one passport. 1:N is like one mother has many children. M:N is like many students take many courses.
